meta.name: Asian-Fusion Stir-Fry Vegetables
meta.subtitle: Crisp-tender vegetables in a fast soy-garlic-ginger sauce (customizable).

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: SSA canonical: weeknight-ready, supports frozen veg, and designed to pair with rice/noodles and any protein.
Sauce includes optional cornstarch for glossy cling; can be made gluten-free with tamari.

summary: A flexible, crisp-tender vegetable stir-fry with a quick soy-garlic-ginger sauce. Works with fresh or frozen vegetables and can be adapted to whatever you have on hand.

yield.notes: Side for 4, or 2–3 as a main over rice/noodles.

optional.label: Pan lid
optional.notes: Helpful for 30–60 seconds of quick steam if using denser vegetables.

notes: Use tamari for gluten-free. If using oyster sauce, note it contains shellfish.

ingredients.label: Broccoli florets
notes: Swap with cauliflower or broccolini if needed.

ingredients.label: Carrots, thinly sliced on a bias
Mushrooms, sliced
notes: Great for extra umami; can omit if unavailable.

ingredients.label: Cabbage, thinly sliced
notes: Napa or green cabbage both work.

ingredients.label: Honey or sugar
notes: Optional; balances salt and acid. Omit for no-sugar versions.

ingredients.label: Cornstarch
notes: Optional for glossy sauce that clings; omit for a lighter finish.

ingredients.label: Red pepper flakes or chili crisp (optional)

steps.title: Prep vegetables and mix sauce
instructions: Cut all vegetables into stir-fry-friendly sizes: bite-size florets, thin carrot slices, and even strips for peppers.
In a small bowl, whisk soy sauce, water, and rice vinegar.
If using cornstarch, whisk it into the sauce until fully dissolved (no lumps).
Set optional finish items (sesame oil, chili, citrus, scallions, sesame seeds) within reach.
checks: Veg sizes should be consistent so they cook evenly.
If using cornstarch, sauce should look smooth, not chalky.

steps.title: Heat pan and bloom aromatics
instructions: Heat a large skillet or wok over medium-high until hot.
Add garlic and ginger and stir constantly for 15–25 seconds until fragrant (do not brown).
checks: Aromatics should smell strong and fragrant; if they start browning, lower heat slightly.

steps.title: Cook dense vegetables first
instructions: Add broccoli and carrots first (and any other dense vegetables). Toss to coat in oil.
Stir-fry 2 minutes. If pan is very dry or veg seems too firm, add 1–2 tbsp water and cover for 30–60 seconds to steam, then uncover.
checks: Broccoli should turn brighter green; carrots should begin to soften but still be crisp.

steps.title: Add remaining vegetables
instructions: Add peppers, snap peas/green beans, mushrooms, and cabbage (if using).
Stir-fry 2–3 minutes until vegetables are crisp-tender.
checks: Vegetables should still have a little bite; avoid overcooking (they continue to soften after saucing).

steps.title: Sauce and glaze
instructions: Give the sauce a quick re-whisk (cornstarch settles fast).
Pour sauce around the edges of the pan and toss continuously.
Cook 30–90 seconds until the sauce lightly thickens and coats the vegetables.
Turn off heat. Stir in sesame oil (if using).
checks: Sauce should cling lightly. If too thick, add 1–2 tbsp water; if too thin, cook 30 seconds longer.

steps.title: Finish and serve
instructions: Taste and adjust: add a squeeze of citrus for brightness or a pinch of salt if needed.
Top with scallions and sesame seeds. Add chili flakes/chili crisp if desired.
Serve immediately as a side, or over rice/noodles as a main.

notes: For larger batches, cook vegetables in 2 rounds to avoid steaming.
Sauce scales linearly; keep cornstarch proportional if using.
Vegetables soften over time; best within 24 hours for crispness.
Freezing makes most stir-fry vegetables watery and soft.

reheat.skillet: Medium-high, 2–3 minutes, stirring; add a splash of water if needed.
reheat.microwave: Cover; 60–90 seconds, stir, then 30–60 seconds more.

notes: Keep vegetables moving in a hot pan to avoid burning garlic/ginger.
If adding meat to the same pan, cook meat to safe internal temperature before adding vegetables.

variations.label: Spicy (Sichuan-ish) Variation
variations.notes: Add 1 tsp chili oil and 1/2 tsp toasted crushed peppercorns at the end; increase vinegar slightly for balance.

variations.label: Citrus Bright Variation
variations.notes: Replace half the water with orange juice and finish with lime; keep sweetness optional.

variations.label: Quick Peanut Sauce Variation
variations.notes: Stir 2 tbsp peanut butter into sauce with an extra splash of water; finish with lime. Note allergen: peanuts.

notes: Frozen vegetable option: use 4–5 cups frozen stir-fry mix; cook hot and fast and expect more water—skip steaming and reduce sauce longer.
If your vegetables are releasing water, crank heat and keep tossing to evaporate quickly.
Cornstarch is optional; for a lighter, glossy finish, reduce sauce a little longer instead.
